Fix Firestore missing indexes and map crash error handling

- Added Google Maps API key and permissions to AndroidManifest.xml
- Created firestore.indexes.json with composite indexes for:
  - listings: category ASC + updatedAt DESC (fixes category filter queries)
  - listings: ownerId ASC + updatedAt DESC (fixes user listings queries)
- Added FlutterError handler in MapViewScreen to catch Maps API key errors
  and show a friendly fallback instead of crashing the app

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Fix: wait for AC.resume() before starting audio channels

Root cause: On iOS Safari, AudioContext.resume() resolves asynchronously.
Oscillators created while AC is still 'suspended' never produce sound.
The fix ensures all audio channels start AFTER AC.resume() resolves.

- togglePlay(): defer _doToggle() to AC.resume().then() callback
- playScene(): wrap all audio startup in startAudio() callback
  that runs only after AC reaches 'running' state

fix: chown repo directory after clone/pull when run with sudo

The git clone/pull runs as root under sudo, creating files owned by
root. This prevents the real user from creating .venv inside the repo.
Fix by chowning the entire repo to the real user immediately after
clone/pull.

docs/ops: Add operational runbooks for secrets and deployment safety

**Problem:** Two critical operational tasks lack precise, step-by-step documentation:
1. Board email secret management (out-of-band, affects disaster recovery)
2. Token hash migration (one-time deployment, must be done correctly)

**Solution: Two Detailed Operational Runbooks**

1. **docs/operational/BOARD_EMAIL_SECRET_RECOVERY.md**
   - Explicit about what's in Git vs out-of-band (BoardEmailConfig.gs)
   - Prerequisites checklist
   - Step-by-step recovery procedure (generate key, create config, test)
   - Verification checklist
   - Rollback procedures
   - Maintainer handoff procedure
   - Disaster recovery workflow
   - Troubleshooting guide

   **Purpose:** Make secret restoration reproducible and safe, reduce handoff risk

2. **docs/operational/TOKEN_HASH_MIGRATION_RUNBOOK.md**
   - Comprehensive preflight checklist (5 validation steps)
   - Exact deployment steps (rename column, run function, push code, test)
   - Verification checklist with success criteria
   - Post-migration verification
   - Validation helper functions
   - Rollback procedures
   - Deployment checklist for ops runbooks
   - 24-hour monitoring guidance

   **Purpose:** Make migration safe, reduce deployment risk, enable confident rollbacks

**Code Changes: Validation Helpers in AuthService.js**

Added three read-only helper functions for post-deployment verification:
1. validateTokenHashMigration() — Check if migration complete
   - Returns: column status, session counts, completion status
   - Use: Run after deployment to verify schema
2. checkSessionFormat(email) — Verify a specific session uses new format
   - Returns: Session format details, token hash length
   - Use: Quick test after login
3. getAuthHealthReport() — Overall auth system health summary
   - Returns: Migration status, session statistics, recommendations
   - Use: Monitor system health and troubleshoot issues

**Documentation Updates: docs/README.md**

Added "Operational Runbooks" section:
- Links to both runbooks
- Explains why board email is intentionally out-of-band
- Notes that token hash migration is one-time deployment
- Points to exact operator-facing steps and checklists

**Impact:**
- Board email secret management now has reproducible recovery procedure
- Token hash migration safe for ops teams (no guessing)
- Validation helpers enable confident verification
- Runbooks prevent "tribal knowledge" loss on handoff
- Checklists reduce deployment errors

**Status:** Ready for ops use before token hash migration deployment

Co-Authored-By: Claude Haiku 4.5 <noreply@anthropic.com>

for active assignment queries. This provides better query performance
and cleaner semantics.

wasn't showing up because the query wasn't finding the assignment.

Add pipeline orchestrator connecting collector to analysis pipeline

- pipeline_orchestrator: four-stage pipeline (cluster → analyze → market
  context → rankings) for a list of article IDs; idempotent per stage —
  existing records are detected and skipped; force_analysis=True triggers
  re-analysis even for unchanged clusters
- article_processing_service: single-article clustering with
  processing_status tracking written back to ArticleRecord
- Idempotency logic: Stage 2 compares cluster.article_count vs
  cluster_event.article_count — re-analyzes only if cluster grew;
  Stage 3 skips ideas that already have MarketContext; Stage 4 deletes
  and rewrites SignalRankings per cluster to keep intra-cluster ranks
  consistent
- ArticleRecord.processing_status column (nullable VARCHAR 32) tracks
  pipeline state per article: null/clustered/duplicate/failed
- migrate_collector_columns extended to add processing_status
- run_collector.py --analyze now triggers the full orchestrator pipeline
  instead of just assign_cluster, printing per-stage counts

Rating tool, cert type, primary sector, and summary bars were showing "—" after resuming a draft because populateReview() ran during goToStep(5) before the async initDataverse() finished loading options. commit.message:
Fix: corriger le mismatch des codes de permission frontend/backend

Cause racine : le frontend utilisait des codes anglais (invoices, quotes,
payments, products, reports, suppliers) alors que le backend génère des codes
français (factures, devis, paiements, produits, rapports, fournisseurs).
Résultat : hasPermission() retournait toujours false → tous les menus et
sections du dashboard étaient invisibles pour tous les utilisateurs non-admin.

- Nouveau fichier permissions.js : constantes PERM centralisées,
  nommées d'après le MODULES list du backend (source de vérité unique)
- Sidebar.jsx : 9 permissions corrigées via PERM.* + section ACHATS ajoutée
- DashboardPage.jsx : 25 appels hasPermission() corrigés via PERM.*

- UTC hour % N 방식에서 마지막 보고서 timestamp 기반으로 변경
  (GitHub Actions cron 지연에 의한 실행 누락 방지)

Co-authored-by: Claude <noreply@anthropic.com>

fix(product-page): add setRequestLocale to fix getTranslations in statically-rendered pages

The product page uses generateStaticParams + revalidate=3600 (static
rendering). next-intl requires setRequestLocale(locale) to be called in
every statically-rendered page before getTranslations — the locale layout
calling it is not sufficient for static pre-rendering contexts.

Without this, next-intl throws "getTranslations is not supported in
Client Components", which crashed the error boundary on every product page.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Add per-line dialogue spatial processing with LUFS normalization and spectral match

- Per-line distance (close/medium/far) and environment (outdoor/indoor/clean) controls
- LUFS-based loudnorm replaces static volume multipliers for cinematic dialogue levels
- Spectral match EQ via FFT bends TTS frequencies toward scene audio profile
- Convolution reverb using impulse responses replaces ffmpeg aecho
- Original SFX bed caching prevents compounding dialogue on re-mix
- Module-level busy state keeps buttons disabled through component remounts
- waitForJob polling ensures UI blocks until background jobs complete

commit.message:
fix(excel): exclude formatting ghost rows from skip count

The rows_skipped count was including empty rows created by Excel cell
formatting (e.g., 984 "empty skipped" for a 16-row table). Now counts
only rows that have actual data in any column, so skip counts reflect
genuine partial/cross-block rows instead of formatting artifacts.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Reduce diagram_data.json from 343MB to 14MB by excluding nav edges

Only body edges (12,692) are included in the payload; 1.57M nav edges
are replaced by per-node nav_inbound/nav_outbound summary counts on
tree nodes. This brings diagram_data.json under GitHub's 100MB limit
so the sitemap page works on GitHub Pages again.

commit.message:
fix: perf-video-card alignment — flex-start prevents short-title misalignment

justify-content:space-between on card body caused huge gaps when title
was short (e.g. single-word caption). Switched to flex-start so title
and stats always stack naturally from top. Added align-items:flex-start
on card so thumb aspect-ratio is respected. Also border-radius on cards.

commit.message:
Classify SpecifyItemSpecparamDecl → §6.20.5 [skip ci]

§6.20.5 "Specify parameters" defines the specparam declaration syntax and semantics, covering its use inside a specify block (e.g., `specparam tPD = 10;`). §30.3 explicitly cross-references §6.20.5 as the authoritative location for specify parameters.
